title: Investigating Soviet History at Helsinki’s Slavonic Library
content:
Investigating Soviet History at Helsinki’s Slavonic Library

leliski@helsinki.fi Fri, 22/05/2026 - 10:43
Upon arriving in Helsinki to start my internship with the CUPOLA project, I was able to visit the renowned Slavonic Library (Slavica) which holds one of the largest collections of materials from Russia in Europe. This text describes my encounter with the library collections and its history as a research hub.

title: Master’s Student Kobe’s farewell
content:
Master’s Student Kobe’s farewell

eaharju@helsinki.fi Thu, 21/05/2026 - 13:28
Kobe Van Cleven, a master’s student from Ghent University in Belgium, ended his four-month research stay in Helsinki with a pizza gathering.

title: Olli Saranko will defend his PhD thesis
content:
Olli Saranko will defend his PhD thesis

mkulmala@helsinki.fi Thu, 21/05/2026 - 10:51

Olli Saranko from FMI will defend his PhD thesis “Modeling of current and projected urban temperatures and related uncertainties in Finnish cities” on Friday 5th June 2026 at 12:00 in Physicum E204.

The opponent is Dr. Natalie Theeuwes from KNMI, the Netherlands, and the Custos is Prof Leena Järvi. 

You are all welcome to attend. There is also possibility to follow online:

title: Miten luontokato pysäytetään tehokkaimmin?
content:
What are the most effective ways to stop biodiversity loss?

lindymar@helsinki.fi Fri, 22/05/2026 - 16:07
Professor of Conservation Biology Heini Kujala studies the effectiveness of different conservation methods and how to integrate information on biodiversity into everyday societal decision-making. Biodiversity loss threatens not only animals and plants but also human health and financial stability.

What are your research topics?

content:
Renewal of Arkadiankatu Bridge will cause changes to traffic arrangements near the Natural History Museum

johanemi@helsinki.fi Wed, 07/01/2026 - 08:40
The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

The renovation will start with the construction of temporary traffic arrangements from Wednesday 7 January onwards. The renovation is expected to be completed in late 2026.

title: Mitä reittiä aivojen viestit kulkevat? Uusi menetelmä etsii vastausta
content:

ekarmits@helsinki.fi Fri, 22/05/2026 - 15:35
Rivers and blood vessels naturally organize into efficient routes. A University of Helsinki researcher applied this principle to the brain and designed a new way to examine brain networks.
